title: One-Positive-Per-Stint Listwise Specialist With Sigmoid-Calibrated Blend
summary: Within a stint, the terminal pre-pit lap is structurally special; CatBoost ranking objectives support within-group pair/list losses, which fit this geometry better than independent row classification.
rationale: Within a stint, the terminal pre-pit lap is structurally special; CatBoost ranking objectives support within-group pair/list losses, which fit this geometry better than independent row classification. For final probabilities, scikit-learn warns isotonic can change AUC via ties, while sigmoid preserves ranking.
implementation_hint: Group rows by `Driver,Race,Stint` and train a ranking specialist where later terminal laps should outrank earlier non-terminal laps in the same stint; blend its OOF logits with the best classifier, then apply only sigmoid/Platt calibration on OOF predictions, optionally per dry vs wet regime.
expected_effect: Can sharpen the model around the single spike before a pit stop while keeping final calibration AUC-safe.
risk: Some stints end without an observed next-lap pit in the available frame, so group construction and label logic must be careful.
